exemplar.title: By using statistics, to what extent is a two-week tennis program effective to improve a 16-year-old girl serving skills in tennis?

detailedExemplarData.title: By using statistics, to what extent is a two-week tennis program effective to improve a 16-year-old girl serving skills in tennis?

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical development: each section builds naturally from introduction through data analysis to conclusion.
Well-organized structure with distinct headings, sub-sections and close placement of figures and commentary.
Title and introduction precisely focus the investigation, guiding the reader from the outset.
weaknesses: Occasional long theoretical digressions and repeated definitions reduce conciseness.
Several tables/figures are misaligned, and some captions are distant from content, interrupting flow.
Inconsistent footnote/bibliography formatting and informal language in conclusion detract from polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place coherence and organization at band 3 (strong logical development and good structure) but conciseness at band 2 (wordiness and minor irrelevancies). Balancing these gives a final score of 3 for Presentation.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, histograms, box plots) to support data exploration.
Correct and consistent use of symbols (Î¼, Ï, z, ÏÂ², CI, hypotheses) throughout most of the text.
Algebraic conversions (meters to kilometres, seconds to hours) are presented accurately and justified.
weaknesses: Transcription errors (mismatched brackets, broken fractions) and occasional LaTeX mistakes reduce clarity.
Misstatement of skewness conditions (inequalities reversed) and inversion of ÏÂ² null hypothesis undermine interpretation.
Unlabeled axes on key plots limit standalone interpretability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the exploration employs appropriate notation and representations (band 3), multiple minor errors in transcription and misstatements impair clarity and consistency. This warrants a score of 2 for Mathematical Communication to reflect effective use but limited precision.

strengths: Significant evidence of independent thinking in selecting and justifying statistical methods for the personalised training programme.
Clear personal approach rooted in the studentâs own tennis experience, driving investigation choices.
Demonstrated initiative by applying multiple tests (skewness methods, ÏÂ² GOF, t-test) and proposing extensions (Spearman rank).
weaknesses: Approach remains fairly conventional, using standard syllabus techniques without highly original innovations.
Personal voice, while present, does not consistently enhance mathematical depth beyond context.
Creativity is solid but not outstanding throughout the entire exploration.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student shows clear personal engagement (band 2) through independent thinking and contextual motivation, but does not reach the outstanding creativity or personal insight needed for band 3. A score of 2 reflects this level.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on data limitations (e.g., smartphone timing, outlier tests) and practical implications of confidence intervals.
Clear critique of outlier methods by comparing two tests and discussing disagreement.
Consistent linking of statistical findings to understanding of serving performance (consistency vs power).
weaknesses: Reflection occasionally remains descriptive without probing deeper methodological or theoretical implications.
Critical analysis is meaningful but stops short of exploring alternative robustness checks (e.g., bootstrapping).
Connections to enhanced understanding are present but not sustained at a strong conceptual level throughout.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments identify meaningful but not deep critical reflection (band 2). The exploration meets criteria for clear, meaningful reflection and analysis but lacks the sustained depth for band 3, justifying a score of 2.

strengths: Appropriate selection and coherent use of HL-level statistical methods (descriptive stats, z-scores, ÏÂ² GOF, t-test, CIs).
Calculations and interpretations are largely correct, with only minor rounding or transcription slips.
Sound understanding of underlying concepts and assumptions, explained clearly with real-world relevance.
weaknesses: Minor errors in rounding and a few transcription mistakes do not materially alter conclusions but indicate room for precision.
Does not incorporate more advanced or non-syllabus techniques (e.g., effect size calculations, coefficient of variation).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All strands of Use of Mathematics align with band 4: relevant HL-level mathematics used effectively, accurate with only minor errors, and good understanding demonstrated. The work does not reach the sophistication of bands 5â6, so a score of 4 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student designs a personalised two-week training programme, chooses which statistical criteria to investigate, and justifies the methodological decisions (e.g. why speed was selected over binomial accuracy).  This shows significant, though not exceptional, independent thinking.

The student demonstrates sound understanding of each statistical concept, explaining assumptions (normality, independence), interpreting outputs and discussing real-world implications.  Misunderstandings are minimal, indicating good understanding throughout rather than thorough, rigorous mastery.

Calculated means, SDs, skewness and test statistics are largely correct; minor rounding slips and a few transcription errors exist but do not materially affect conclusions.  Hence accuracy is good with only minor errors, aligning with band 4.

Most material directly addresses the research question, yet the narrative is wordy: long theoretical digressions (e.g. history of Pearsonâs coefficient, repeated definitions of CLT, extended explanation of Sturgesâ rule) add length without advancing the argument.  These redundancies reduce conciseness, but irrelevancies are not dominant, so the strand fits âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements.â

Symbols for mean (Î¼), standard deviation (Ï), z, ÏÂ², CI and hypotheses are used correctly; notation for intervals and inequalities is appropriate.  Occasional LaTeX transcription errors (e.g. missing \frac braces) and some mixing of decimal/ratio forms keep it from complete accuracy, but the mathematical language is largely appropriate throughout.

Explanations generally match the notation and diagrams provided, and the line of reasoning is easy to follow.  A few transcription issues (mismatched brackets, broken fractions) cause minor inconsistencies, but overall clarity and consistency are maintained across the exploration.

All mathematics employedâdescriptive statistics, z-scores, ÏÂ² GOF, t-test, confidence intervalsâis firmly at AI HL course level and chosen appropriately for the question.  The methods are applied in a logically coherent sequence, constituting effective use, though they do not extend into more advanced or non-syllabus techniques that would merit bands 5-6.

The student employs tables, box-and-whisker plots, histograms and numerical summaries to represent data, and combines these with algebraic calculations.  These multiple representations support the argument effectively, yet remain routine rather than âsophisticatedâ (e.g. no dynamic simulation or layered graphics), so the work sits comfortably in the âeffectiveâ category.

The student reflects on data limitations, the impact of outliers, and the practical meaning of confidence intervals, demonstrating meaningful reflection.  However, the commentary stays descriptive at times and does not probe deeper implications (e.g. reliability of smartphone timing) with sustained critical depth.

Selecting, justifying and applying several statistical tests (two skewness methods, ÏÂ² GOF, two-sample t-test) shows initiative and some creativity.  The methods are standard syllabus techniques, so the level of originality is significant but not outstanding.

The report follows a clear logical path: introduction of the question, data collection, processing, analysis, comparison and conclusion.  Each stage builds naturally on the previous one and the reader can see how the statistical tools answer the research question.  Minor breaks in flow (occasional long digressions on theory and repeated explanations) prevent it from reaching an âexcellentâ level, but overall coherence is strong.

There is clear analysis of two different outlier tests, discussion of why they disagree, and evaluation of standard deviation changes.  This shows meaningful critique, yet the reasoning occasionally accepts calculations without exploring alternative models or robustness, so it falls short of âdeep critical analysis.â

The exploration frequently links statistical results to understanding of serving performance (e.g. consistency vs power, impact on double faults), illustrating a clear connection between mathematics and tennis ability.  The link is thoughtful but not consistently pursued to enhance conceptual understanding at every stage.

There is an evident introduction, substantial body with sub-sections (data, descriptive statistics, normality checks, hypothesis test) and a short conclusion.  Headings and appendices help navigation, and diagrams are placed close to the commentary.  A few tables/figures are mis-aligned and some appendix material interrupts the main text, so the organisation is not entirely seamless, but it is well above âadequateâ.

The investigation is strongly rooted in the studentâs personal tennis experience; the narrative consistently refers to her own performance and training context, driving the mathematical work.  While personal voice is evident, the approach remains fairly conventional, hence âclear personal approachâ rather than âhighly personal.â

annotations.comment: The formulas converting meters to kilometres and seconds to hours are presented correctly and consistently.
Why is the central limit theorem used to assume normality but then the z-value test for outliers relies on normality? Clarify the logical consistency of these assumptions.
The chi-square testâs null hypothesis is inverted: Hâ should posit normality. This misstatement undermines the testâs interpretation.
The discussion of increased dispersion after training shows thoughtful real-world interpretation linking statistics to performance variability.
Consider computing the coefficient of variation (Ï/Î¼) for before and after datasets to better compare relative variability.
The conclusion uses informal language (âmy father always told meâ), which detracts from academic tone and presentation polish.
It would strengthen the statistical argument to report an effect size (e.g., Cohenâs d) alongside p-values for the t-test results.
The bibliography entries use inconsistent formatting and lack standard citation structure, reducing clarity of sources.
The title is clear, concise and sets a precise focus for the study, guiding the reader immediately to the research question.
The introduction effectively personalizes the investigation by linking to the studentâs experience, but the narrative is overly wordy and repeats definitions, reducing conciseness.
The decision to use convenience sampling to control fatigue effects is noted, but the impact of non-probability sampling on generalizability should be critically examined.
Explanation of the measurement procedure is clear, but key metadata (e.g. video frame rate) is omitted, which may affect the accuracy of time measurements.
The discussion of smartphone timing and re-alignment limitations shows awareness of data quality issues and valuable reflection on method constraints.
To deepen critical analysis, propose concrete mitigation strategies (e.g. calibration markers) to reduce measuring tape misalignment.
Several tables are misaligned and captions are distant from their content, which interrupts the logical flow of the data presentation.
Rounding serve speeds to whole numbers is well justified by sporting conventions, ensuring results remain interpretable by non-technical audiences.
The box plot is an effective representation, but the axes are unlabeled and the scale is unclear, reducing the plotâs standalone interpretability.
Using both numerical summaries and box plots shows an effective use of multiple representations to explore data distribution.
The derivation of Pearsonâs second coefficient is clear and the formula $$SK_2=\frac{3(\mu-M_d)}{\sigma}$$ is correctly displayed.
The footnote style is inconsistent with the main bibliography, and HTML artifacts (e.g., <sup>) appear, affecting presentation polish.
The conditions for skewness interpretation are stated incorrectly (inequalities reversed), leading to potential confusion in classification.
Consider including kernel density estimates or QQ-plots to complement histograms for assessing normality more sensitively.
The proposed extension using Spearman rank demonstrates outstanding initiative and personal engagement with alternative methods.
The studentâs choice of tests (skewness, ÏÂ² GOF, IQR rule, t-test) shows significant independent thinking in method selection.
To enhance reflection on statistical assumptions, consider bootstrapping confidence intervals to assess robustness to distributional violations.
